data.title: Illumina names former NIH official as medical chief; commercial chief joins Quanterix
data.description:
Illumina (ILMN) shares fell on Friday after its chief commercial officer, Everett Cunningham, resigned to lead the rival life sciences company Quanterix (QTRX), while former NIH genomics director Eric Green joined its C-suite as chief medical officer.  

In a press release late Thursday, the San Diego, California-based gene sequencing company announced that Green, who has led the National Human Genome Research Institute at NIH for more than a decade, will assume his new role effective Feb. 2.

"Eric is a once-in-a-generation leader in genomics whose career has closely tracked the evolution of the field itself," said CEO Jacob Thaysen, who is scheduled to present at this year’s J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on Jan. 13.

Meanwhile, Quanterix (QTRX) announced that Cunningham, who has served as Illumina's (ILMN) commercial chief since June 2024, will become its President and CEO and a member of the Board, effective Jan. 16.

He will replace Masoud Toloue, who has led the Billerica, Massachusetts-based company for more than five years. Quanterix (QTRX) shares surged in reaction as the company concurrently announced that it will exceed its revenue and cash guidance for 2025.

data.title: Wall Street rises after jobs report, no SCOTUS ruling on tariffs
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Friday as the key jobs report came in line with expectations and the U.S. Supreme Court did not rule on President Donald Trump's tariffs.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.5% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.7%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

The indexes are on track for weekly gains after a week of losses.

The Supreme Court was believed to release its ruling on the legality of Trump’s tariffs on Friday, but a decision did not come in. The court could issue additional opinions in the upcoming weeks.

Also on focus today, the December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

“Friday's weaker-than-expected jobs report showed that hiring continued to slow towards the end of 2025, capping a tough year for the labor market. Although the market is still questioning the soundness of the jobs data given the recent disruptions from the government shutdown, throughout 2025, the labor market faced many challenges, including uncertainties from tariffs to artificial intelligence,” said Dennis Follmer, CIO at Montis Financial.

“Higher for longer on interest rates has been the name of the game for an economy continuing to surprise to the upside. As long as that economic strength continues, the market’s expectations for rate cuts are likely to prove overly optimistic,” Follmer said.

Also, the January consumer sentiment index also came in slightly higher than expected and higher than December, according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped 1 basis point to 4.17%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.52%.

On geopolitics, Trump said he has called off plans for a second wave of military attacks on Venezuela, citing the release of political prisoners and calling it "a very important and smart gesture."

data.title: Sandisk surges after report indicating company could double price of 3D NAND for enterprise SSDs: report
data.description:
Sandisk (SNDK) could double the price of its high-capacity 3D NAND memory devices for enterprise-grade solid-state drives this quarter, anticipating strong demand for server-class storage in the coming quarters, Tom's Hardware reported, citing Nomura Securities.

It is not clear to what extent the price increase of high-capacity 3D NAND will affect quotes on mainstream flash memory used in client devices, the report added.

Sandisk did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Channel checks indicate that several memory suppliers continued to push prices higher, with enterprise-grade NAND facing especially aggressive increases," the analysts noted. "SanDisk’s NAND used in enterprise SSDs is cited as potentially rising by more than 100% quarter over quarter in the March period."

Nomura thinks that the plans of memory suppliers to raise prices of enterprise-grade 3D NAND both to short-term shortages and to mid-term growth of demand, driven by AI in general and changes to AI storage in particular, according to the report.

Nomura names Nvidia's (NVDA) Inference Context Memory Storage Platform, or ICMSP, which is based on the BlueField-4 DPU equipped with a 512 GB SSD that carries KV cache, among the demand drivers for enterprise storage this year, the report noted.

Shares of SanDisk jumped about 11% on Friday, while stocks of other memory product makers also surged. Micron (MU) rose 4%, Seagate Technology (STX) surged nearly 5%, while Western Digital (WDC) climbed around 3%.

Earlier this week, it was reported that Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK hynix (HXSC.F) are looking to raise prices for server memory by up to 70% in the first quarter, while a surging demand in AI impacts global supply.

data.title: Cleveland-Cliffs upgraded on transformation opportunity; Nucor, Steel Dynamics cut at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) +5.1% in Friday's trading as Morgan Stanley upgraded to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $17 price target, raised from $12.80, while Nucor (NUE) and Steel Dynamics (STLD) tilt lower as the bank lowered its ratings for the steelmakers to Equal Weight from Overweight with respective $180 and $194 price targets.

Morgan Stanley analyst Carlos De Alba said the near-term backdrop offers few obvious cyclical tailwinds for the North American steel industry, with a 50% steel tariff in place, soft demand, and steel prices already elevated, but Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) is especially well positioned as the only domestic producer capable of "true transformation" via a potential win-win partnership with Korea's Posco (PKX).

Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) is operating with a highly leveraged balance sheet with interest expense expected to exceed $600M this year, and its cost structure has constrained its financial upside, as reflected in an estimated $1.3B net loss in 2025 despite healthy steel prices, but De Alba said a strategic transaction with Posco (PKX) has the potential to address both issues.

De Alba also downgraded Nucor (NUE) and Steel Dynamics (STLD), as both steelmakers have balanced risk-rewards with limited upside; elevated steel prices may continue for now, but amid soft demand conditions, the risk to prices is slightly skewed to the downside.

The analyst also noted that Steel Dynamics (STLD) boasts industry-leading free cash flow and strong inflection in the coming years, but some of the cash may be spent on the recent bid for BlueScope Steel instead of being returned to shareholders.

data.title: Youlife Group forms cruise talent joint venture with Sealand Maritime
data.description:
• Youlife Group (YOUL) on Friday said that it has entered into a joint venture with Sealand Maritime Service, to develop a talent training and placement platform for the global cruise and cultural tourism industries.
  • The two companies have established Xiamen Youlife Sealand International Cultural Tourism Development Co, which will focus on vocational training, certification and international job placement for cruise service professionals.
  • The partnership comes as international cruise operations in China have resumed and demand for trained cruise staff has increased.
  • YOUL is -0.0% to $1.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump shared some December jobs data hours before official release
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump posted a chart containing unpublished December jobs data on his social media platform roughly 12 hours before the figures were set to be made public Friday morning.

The president shared a chart on Truth Social showing the private sector added 654K jobs since the start of the year, while federal government jobs were down year-to-date.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics released its nonfarm payrolls report on Friday at 8:30 a.m. ET, showing 50K jobs added in 2025's last month, roughly in line with the consensus and slightly slower than the prior month's print. Trump did not publish the actual December payroll number, though his post may have signaled to investors how the jobs report would come in.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Compass, Anywhere Real Estate avoided deal probe amid Justice Dept. rift - WSJ
data.description:
Justice Department antitrust officials wanted to investigate the $1.6 billion merger of Compass Inc. (COMP) and Anywhere Real Estate, but were overruled by senior officials who felt the deal should be approved.

Gail Slater, the head of the DOJ's antitrust division, wanted an extended review of the real estate brokerage deal, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Compass and its lawyers appealed above her, to the office of Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, saying that any concerns could be addressed without an investigation. Blanche's office agreed. 

Compass and Anywhere declined to comment to the WSJ. A spokeswoman for Blanche said the department "complied with its obligations" under antitrust law to review the deal.

Compass (COMP) and Anywhere closed their deal on Friday.

data.title: Goldman Sachs cuts iHeartMedia to Sell as digital shift accelerates
data.description:
As advertisers migrate towards digital platforms and with audio consumption declining, iHeartMedia (IHRT) was downgraded to Sell at Goldman Sachs, driving shares further into the red to a 2-month low.

The firm also lowered its target price for the stock by 12.5% to a street low of $3.50.

IHeartMedia (IHRT) operates in three segments: Multiplatform Group, which accounts for 60% of revenue and is the company’s traditional radio business; Digital Audio Group, which accounts for 30% of revenue; and Audio & Video Media Services Group, which accounts for the remaining 10%. Of the three, the Multiplatform Group has been on a downward trajectory amid a shift away from broadcast radio to internet audio.

“Globally, broadcast radio has been declining as audiences, notably younger ones, shifted to digital platforms. Further declines may be possible going forward due to audience attrition as legacy listeners age out of the market. Around 25% of America’s population is aged 50 and over, and it is likely, considering current consumer preferences, that this loss will not be replaced by an equally engaged younger audience,” says Seeking Alpha analyst LD Investments.

Adds Goldman, “the continued rollout of connected in-car infotainment systems and increased penetration of audio services will be headwinds to AM/FM audio engagement.”

Besides a shift away from terrestrial radio, Goldman also warns of the expansion of artificial intelligence in the category that will introduce levels of control, curation, and discoverability to audio that will “further incentivize listeners to migrate from broadcast to digital audio service which are better suited to deliver AI capabilities.”

This is also true for advertisers, giving them the ability to personalize, target and enhance the efficiency of their ad budgets.

Looking ahead to the two most important advertising catalysts in 2026 – the FIFA World Cup and mid-term elections – broadcast radio/iHeartMedia will still benefit from these events, but Goldman warns that the scope of that benefit will be narrower compared to other formats.

Lastly, with $5B of debt maturing in 2031, free cash flow generation presents another worry for investors.

Although the company can refinance this debt, “it remains to be seen whether management can exchange this debt at a better rate,” LD Investments adds.

“My base case scenario, therefore, assumes a step up in interest payments (as much as a 30% increase from current levels, i.e. over $350M) along with higher interest rates.”

iHeartMedia (IHTR) shares are down 4.4% on Friday.

data.title: AllianceBernstein sees steady growth in 2026, but flags rising volatility and policy risks
data.description:
AllianceBernstein’s global macro outlook for the first quarter of 2026 strikes a cautiously constructive tone, emphasizing steady economic expansion alongside rising sources of uncertainty. The firm expects global growth to continue through the coming quarters at a pace sufficient to support financial markets, even if it falls short of historical averages.

At the same time, the investment firm highlights meaningful downside risks emerging from both policy decisions and underlying economic conditions. In the United States, the Federal Reserve faces a particularly complex backdrop the firm noted. A gradually cooling labor market combined with inflation that remains stubbornly above target complicates the path forward, leaving policymakers with no clearly optimal choice. 

From a structural perspective, artificial intelligence ((AI)) remains a long-term positive for productivity, but the investment firm cautions that such gains typically materialize slowly. Markets, however, may be less willing to wait for tangible evidence in the form of sustained earnings growth, particularly given the elevated valuations across many AI-related assets. 

Looking ahead, AllianceBernstein expects market conditions to become more volatile in 2026 after an unusually calm second half of 2025. With asset prices already reflecting a significant amount of optimism, the firm advises investors to temper return expectations.

Even favorable economic developments, it notes, may not translate into outsized gains in an environment where much of the good news is already priced in.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Insmed gains as preliminary 2025 revenue beats consensus
data.description:
Insmed (INSM) shares traded higher on Friday after the rare disease drug developer announced preliminary unaudited revenue for 2025, exceeding Wall Street forecasts ahead of a presentation at the upcoming Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.

The Bridgewater, New Jersey-based biotech expects its full-year revenue to reach $606.4M, indicating ~67% YoY growth compared to $522.0M in the consensus, thanks mainly to $453.0M of U.S. revenue, which grew ~78% YoY.

Its key revenue generators, Arikayce and Brinsupri, have generated $172.7M and $433.8M to the topline, respectively, with Brinsupri adding $144.6M during Q4, its full quarter of commercial rollout.

Meanwhile, Arikayce, indicated for mycobacterium avium complex ((MAC)) lung disease, exceeded the upper end of the company’s prior revenue guidance, Insmed (INSM) said.

As for upcoming catalysts, topline readouts from the company’s Phase 3 ENCORE study of Arikayce in newly diagnosed or recurrent MAC lung disease and the Phase 2b CEDAR study of Brensocatib in hidradenitis suppurativa are now expected in March or April and Q2 2026, respectively.  

Insmed’s (INSM) JPM presentation is scheduled to take place on Jan. 12 at 6:00 p.m. ET.
